Earlier in our training, we mentioned that your EQ was indispensable for building trust-based influence. It's also essential in navigating the seemingly irrational patterns of thinking your counterpart might have that could create obstacles. What do I mean by this? While people may often seem irrational, they're not unpredictable. Human behavior shares similar patterns across all cultures. When you know the human nature patterns behind your counterpart's thinking, you're aligning your product or service to them, not to you or what you think they need. You are bending the reality of your counterpart so that your deal resonates with them. The psychologists Daniel Kahneman and Amos Tversky developed the best explanation for defining the patterns of our irrational decisions. They called it prospect theory, and it was so revolutionary that it won the Nobel Prize for Behavioral Economics. The idea behind prospect theory is that it describes how people choose between options that involve risk. Within prospect theory, there is the certainty effect, which argues that people are more likely to choose certainty over probability, even if the probability is a better choice. There's a book out about sales that's done very well called Predictable Revenue. Note, it's not called Maximizing Revenue. In addition to the certainty effect, there is also the effect of loss aversion. This tells us that individuals would take greater risks to avoid taking a loss than to achieve a gain. For example, it says that losing $5 hurts twice as much as gaining $5 feels good. There's data that exists that says about 70% of buy decisions are made to avoid losses, not to achieve gains. What does this mean? Well, it means that it isn't enough to demonstrate to your counterpart that you can deliver a gain. Your edge comes when you get them to grasp what they will lose by doing nothing. And you can't do this effectively without first using tactical empathy, or you will seem like a threat. Now, I'm going to go over a few helpful tactics that you can use to your advantage next time you find yourself in a tough negotiation. Click on the options to learn about these tactics.
